fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: South Dakota Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to South Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity south dakota, how to transfer occupational therapist license to south dakota, occupational therapist compact south dakota, south dakota occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners — OT
board.mailing_address: 101 N Main Ave, Suite 301, Sioux Falls, SD 57104

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (by July 1st of even-numbered years)
quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) exam

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in South Dakota.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create Account & Start Application
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the MyLicense Online Portal at https://sdbmoe.mylicense.com/ and select the 'Occupational Therapist Application (Initial and Endorsement)'.

steps.title: Complete Online Application & Upload Documents
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form on the MyLicense Online Portal and upload necessary documents, including the Declaration of Primary State of Residence Form.

steps.title: Arrange for Supporting Documentation Submission
steps.description: Request official transcripts from your educational institution and your NBCOT score report to be sent directly to the South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners. If endorsing, arrange for Verification of Licensure from other states to be sent directly from the issuing boards.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprint Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the Board to complete the required fingerprint background check. This involves obtaining the necessary forms from the Board's website and submitting your fingerprints.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee of $100.00 through the MyLicense Online Portal at the time of submission.

steps.title: Submit Application and Await Review
steps.description: Submit your completed application through the MyLicense Online Portal. The Board will review your application once all required materials, including direct submissions, are received. The Board meets monthly for review.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: South Dakota offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners — OT for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: South Dakota is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in South Dakota without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, South Dakota also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

faqs.question: How much does a South Dakota OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $100. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does South Dakota have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: South Dakota has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to South Dakota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: South Dakota is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in South Dakota without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, South Dakota also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

hero.title: Become an Occupational Therapist in South Dakota
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in South Dakota, applicants must utilize the South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners' MyLicense Online Portal to complete and submit their application.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the MyLicense Online Portal. Click 'Register a New User' to create an account with a valid email address and password. Once registered, log in to start a new application for Occupational Therapist licensure.
